member_findings.title: Success-rate predicate is inverted
member_findings.impact: The tracker claims to check for a success rate below the threshold, but this line returns true when successCount is greater than total * ratio. With the default 0.5 threshold, a fully healthy window triggers worker failover while a 0% success window does not, so persistent URMA data-plane failures leave the client pinned to the bad worker and healthy traffic can cause unnecessary switches.
member_findings.fix: Change the predicate to strict less-than, for example `return static_cast<double>(snapshot.successCount) < static_cast<double>(total) * successRateRatio;`, and keep the equality case non-triggering.
